зҺ°еңЁжҲ‘еҜ№ж•ҙдёӘйЎ№зӣ®жһ¶жһ„жңүдәҶе…Ёйқўзҡ„зҗҶи§ЈпјҢдёӢйқўжқҘиҜҰз»ҶеҲҶжһҗе°Ҷ session еҒҡжҲҗ chat йЎөйқў tab зҡ„еҸҜиЎҢжҖ§е’Ңе·ҘдҪңйҮҸгҖӮ

---

## е°Ҷ Session еҒҡжҲҗ Chat йЎөйқў Tab зҡ„еҸҜиЎҢжҖ§еҲҶжһҗ

### дёҖгҖҒеҪ“еүҚжһ¶жһ„жҖ»з»“

| еұӮзә§ | зҺ°зҠ¶ |
|------|------|
| **еёғеұҖ** | е·Ұдҫ§ SessionSidebarпјҲж ‘еҪўпјү + еҸідҫ§ ChatViewпјҲеҚ•дјҡиҜқпјү |
| **зҠ¶жҖҒз®ЎзҗҶ** | `usePiRpc` з®ЎзҗҶж¶ҲжҒҜжөҒпјҢ`useSessionManager` з®ЎзҗҶдјҡиҜқеҲ—иЎЁ |
| **Pi еҗҺз«Ҝ** | еҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘ `AgentSession`пјҢ`runtime.switchSession()` еҲҮжҚў |
| **еҲҮжҚўдјҡиҜқ** | дёІиЎҢпјҡ`switchSession` вҶ’ `clearMessages` вҶ’ `loadHistory`пјҢжңүзҪ‘з»ңе»¶иҝҹ |
| **ж¶ҲжҒҜеӯҳеӮЁ** | дјҡиҜқж¶ҲжҒҜд»Һ Pi worker е®һж—¶жӢүеҸ–пјҲ`loadHistory`пјүпјҢж— еүҚз«Ҝжң¬ең°зј“еӯҳ |

### дәҢгҖҒTab жЁЎејҸзҡ„и®ҫи®Ўж–№жЎҲ

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ [Tab: main] [Tab: refactor] [Tab: bugfix] [+]в”Ӯ  вҶҗ ж–° Tab ж Ҹ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ              еҪ“еүҚ Tab зҡ„ ChatView             в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                              в”Ӯ
в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
в”Ӯ              InputBar                        в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### дёүгҖҒеҸҜиЎҢжҖ§еҲӨж–ӯпјҡ**еҸҜиЎҢпјҢдҪҶжңүж ёеҝғйҡңзўҚ**

#### вң… еҸҜиЎҢзҡ„йғЁеҲҶ

1. **UI еұӮж”№йҖ **пјҡReact з»„д»¶жӢҶеҲҶеҠ дёҖдёӘ Tab ж ҸеҫҲзӣҙжҺҘпјҢжҠҖжңҜдёҠж— йҡңзўҚ
2. **Session еҲ—иЎЁе·Іжңү**пјҡ`useSessionManager` е·ІжҸҗдҫӣ `sessions` ж•°жҚ®пјҢTab еҸӘйңҖж¶Ҳиҙ№е®ғ
3. **еҗҺз«Ҝ IPC йҖҡйҒ“е®ҢеӨҮ**пјҡ`switchSession`/`newSession`/`forkAtEntry` зӯү RPC е·Іе…ЁйғЁжү“йҖҡ

#### вҡ пёҸ ж ёеҝғйҡңзўҚ

| йҡңзўҚ | дёҘйҮҚзЁӢеәҰ | иҜҙжҳҺ |
|------|----------|------|
| **Pi еҸӘж”ҜжҢҒдёҖдёӘжҙ»и·ғ Session** | рҹ”ҙ й«ҳ | `pi-worker.ts` дёӯеҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘе…ЁеұҖ `session` е’Ң `runtime`пјҢеҲҮжҚўдјҡиҜқеҝ…йЎ» `switchSession()`пјҢиҝҷжҳҜдёҖдёӘејӮжӯҘж“ҚдҪңпјҢйңҖиҰҒ Pi еҶ…йғЁе…ій—ӯж—§ sessionгҖҒеҠ иҪҪж–° session |
| **Tab еҲҮжҚўжңүе»¶иҝҹ** | рҹ”ҙ й«ҳ | жҜҸж¬Ў Tab еҲҮжҚў = `switchSession` + `clearMessages` + `loadHistory`пјҢж¶үеҸҠ Pi RPC еҫҖиҝ”пјҢдҪ“ж„ҹеҸҜиғҪеңЁ 200-500msпјҢж— жі•еҒҡеҲ°жөҸи§ҲеҷЁ Tab йӮЈз§ҚеҚіж—¶еҲҮжҚў |
| **ж¶ҲжҒҜж— еүҚз«Ҝзј“еӯҳ** | рҹҹЎ дёӯ | еҪ“еүҚ `usePiRpc` еҸӘз»ҙжҠӨдёҖдёӘ `messages[]`пјҢеҲҮ Tab е°ұдёўеӨұж—§ Tab зҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҢйңҖиҰҒдёәжҜҸдёӘ Tab зј“еӯҳж¶ҲжҒҜ |
| **жөҒејҸе“Қеә”еҶІзӘҒ** | рҹҹЎ дёӯ | еҰӮжһң Tab A жӯЈеңЁ streamingпјҢеҲҮжҚўеҲ° Tab BпјҢstreaming дәӢд»¶иҝҳйңҖиҰҒжӯЈзЎ®и·Ҝз”ұеҲ° Tab A зҡ„зј“еӯҳ |
| **Session ж ‘з»“жһ„дёўеӨұ** | рҹҹЎ дёӯ | еҪ“еүҚ Sidebar еұ•зӨәзҡ„жҳҜж ‘еҪў fork е…ізі»пјҲparent/childпјүпјҢTab жҳҜзәҝжҖ§зҡ„пјҢеҰӮдҪ•иЎЁиҫҫж ‘з»“жһ„жҳҜдёӘи®ҫи®Ўй—®йўҳ |

### еӣӣгҖҒиҜҰз»Ҷе·ҘдҪңйҮҸиҜ„дј°

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 1пјҡеҹәзЎҖ Tab UIпјҲ1-2 еӨ©пјү

- ж–°е»ә `SessionTabBar` з»„д»¶
- дҝ®ж”№ `App.tsx` еёғеұҖпјҡз§»йҷӨе·Ұдҫ§ SidebarпјҢеңЁ ChatView дёҠж–№еҠ  Tab ж Ҹ
- Tab зҡ„жҳҫзӨәпјҡsession name + е…ій—ӯжҢүй’® + ж–°е»әжҢүй’®
- жҙ»и·ғ Tab й«ҳдә®гҖҒзӮ№еҮ»еҲҮжҚў

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 2пјҡж¶ҲжҒҜеӨҡ Tab зј“еӯҳпјҲ2-3 еӨ©пјүвҶҗ ж ёеҝғйҡҫзӮ№

еҪ“еүҚ `usePiRpc` жҳҜеҚ•дјҡиҜқзҠ¶жҖҒжңәгҖӮйңҖиҰҒйҮҚжһ„дёәпјҡ

```typescript
// йңҖиҰҒз»ҙжҠӨзҡ„ж•°жҚ®з»“жһ„
interface TabState {
  sessionPath: string
  messages: ChatMessage[]
  isStreaming: boolean
  streamingMessageId: string | null
  tokenUsage: TokenUsage
  forkPoints: ForkPoint[]
  scrollPosition?: number
}

// е…ЁеұҖ Tab з®ЎзҗҶ
const [tabs, setTabs] = useState<Map<string, TabState>>(new Map())
const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<string | null>(null)
```

е…·дҪ“ж”№еҠЁпјҡ
- `usePiRpc` вҶ’ жӢҶеҲҶдёә**дәӢд»¶и·Ҝз”ұеұӮ** + **еӨҡ Tab зҠ¶жҖҒеұӮ**
- дәӢд»¶и·Ҝз”ұпјҡPi жҺЁжқҘзҡ„ `agent_start`/`message_start`/`text_delta` зӯүйңҖиҰҒжӯЈзЎ®еҲҶеҸ‘еҲ°еҜ№еә” Tab зҡ„зј“еӯҳ
- `currentAssistantId` / `currentContentBlocks` йңҖиҰҒжҢү Tab йҡ”зҰ»
- дј°з®—пјҡ**300-500 иЎҢд»Јз Ғж”№еҠЁ**пјҢж ёеҝғжҳҜ `usePiRpc.ts` зҡ„йҮҚжһ„

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 3пјҡTab еҲҮжҚўйҖ»иҫ‘пјҲ1-2 еӨ©пјү

- зӮ№еҮ» Tab вҶ’ и°ғз”Ё `switchSession` RPC вҶ’ зӯүеҫ…жҲҗеҠҹ вҶ’ жҒўеӨҚзј“еӯҳ
- еҲҮжҚўж—¶зҡ„ loading зҠ¶жҖҒпјҲеӣ дёә Pi switchSession жңүе»¶иҝҹпјү
- жҒўеӨҚж»ҡеҠЁдҪҚзҪ®
- йҰ–ж¬Ўжү“ејҖ TabпјҲзј“еӯҳдёӯжІЎжңүпјүйңҖиҰҒ `loadHistory`

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 4пјҡStreaming и·Ё Tab и·Ҝз”ұпјҲ1-2 еӨ©пјү

- Tab A streaming дёӯпјҢз”ЁжҲ·еҲҮеҲ° Tab B
- Pi зҡ„дәӢд»¶з»§з»ӯжҺЁйҖҒз»ҷ Tab A зҡ„зј“еӯҳ
- Tab A зҡ„ Tab ж ҮйўҳйңҖиҰҒжҳҫзӨә streaming жҢҮзӨәеҷЁпјҲеҰӮж—ӢиҪ¬еӣҫж Үпјү
- йңҖиҰҒзӣ‘еҗ¬йқһжҙ»и·ғ Tab зҡ„ `agent_end` дәӢд»¶жқҘжӣҙж–°зҠ¶жҖҒ

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 5пјҡTab е…ій—ӯ + иҫ№з•ҢеӨ„зҗҶпјҲ1 еӨ©пјү

- е…ій—ӯ Tab вҶ’ зЎ®и®ӨеҜ№иҜқжЎҶ вҶ’ `deleteSession` жҲ–д»…е…ій—ӯ UI
- е…ій—ӯжӯЈеңЁ streaming зҡ„ Tab вҶ’ е…Ҳ `abort`
- жңҖеҗҺдёҖдёӘ Tab е…ій—ӯ вҶ’ иҮӘеҠЁеҲӣе»әж–° session
- Tab ж•°йҮҸйҷҗеҲ¶пјҲйҒҝе…ҚеҶ…еӯҳй—®йўҳпјү

#### йҳ¶ж®ө 6пјҡSidebar дёҺ Tab зҡ„е…ұеӯҳ/еҸ–иҲҚпјҲ1-2 еӨ©пјү

и®ҫи®ЎеҶізӯ–пјҡ
- **ж–№жЎҲ A**пјҡе®Ңе…Ёз§»йҷӨ SidebarпјҢз”Ё Tab д»Јжӣҝ вҶ’ з®ҖеҚ•дҪҶдёўеӨұж ‘еҪўз»“жһ„
- **ж–№жЎҲ B**пјҡдҝқз•ҷ Sidebar дҪңдёәеҸҜжҠҳеҸ йқўжқҝпјҢTab еҸӘжҳҫзӨәжңҖиҝ‘жү“ејҖзҡ„ вҶ’ еӨҚжқӮдҪҶдҝқз•ҷе®Ңж•ҙдҝЎжҒҜ
- **ж–№жЎҲ C**пјҡTab еҸӘжҳҫзӨәеҗҢзә§еҲ«зҡ„ sibling sessionsпјҢж ‘еҪўе…ізі»д»ҚеңЁ Sidebar вҶ’ жҠҳдёӯ

### дә”гҖҒжҖ»е·ҘдҪңйҮҸ

| еңәжҷҜ | е·ҘдҪңйҮҸ | дҪ“йӘҢ |
|------|--------|------|
| **жңҖз®Җж–№жЎҲ**пјҡTab UI + еҗҢжӯҘеҲҮжҚўпјҲж— зј“еӯҳпјҢеҲҮ Tab е°ұ reloadпјү | 2-3 еӨ© | еҲҮжҚўжңүе»¶иҝҹпјҢдҪ“йӘҢе·® |
| **дёӯзӯүж–№жЎҲ**пјҡTab UI + ж¶ҲжҒҜзј“еӯҳ + жөҒејҸи·Ҝз”ұ | 6-8 еӨ© | еҲҮжҚўеҚіж—¶пјҢstreaming еҸҜи·Ё Tab |
| **е®Ңж•ҙж–№жЎҲ**пјҡдёӯзӯүж–№жЎҲ + Sidebar е…ұеӯҳ + ж ‘еҪў Tab еұ•зӨә | 9-12 еӨ© | еҠҹиғҪе®Ңж•ҙпјҢи®ҫи®ЎеӨҚжқӮ |

### е…ӯгҖҒе»әи®®

1. **е…ҲеҒҡеҺҹеһӢйӘҢиҜҒе»¶иҝҹ**пјҡеңЁеҠЁе·ҘеүҚпјҢе…ҲжөӢйҮҸ `switchSession` + `loadHistory` зҡ„з«ҜеҲ°з«Ҝе»¶иҝҹгҖӮеҰӮжһң < 100msпјҢжңҖз®Җж–№жЎҲе°ұеӨҹз”ЁпјӣеҰӮжһң > 300msпјҢеҝ…йЎ»еҒҡж¶ҲжҒҜзј“еӯҳгҖӮ

2. **жҺЁиҚҗдёӯзӯүж–№жЎҲ**пјҡTab жЁЎејҸзҡ„ж ёеҝғд»·еҖјжҳҜгҖҢеҚіж—¶еҲҮжҚўгҖҚпјҢеҰӮжһңдёҚеҒҡзј“еӯҳе°ұеӨұеҺ»дәҶж„Ҹд№үгҖӮ6-8 еӨ©жҠ•е…ҘеҗҲзҗҶгҖӮ

3. **Tab дёҺ Sidebar зҡ„е…ізі»**пјҡе»әи®®дҝқз•ҷ Sidebar дҪңдёә session з®ЎзҗҶйқўжқҝпјҲforkгҖҒйҮҚе‘ҪеҗҚгҖҒж ‘еҪўеұ•зӨәпјүпјҢTab еҸӘеұ•зӨәгҖҢжү“ејҖзҡ„дјҡиҜқгҖҚгҖӮиҝҷи·ҹ VS Code зҡ„ Explorer + Tab жЁЎејҸзұ»дјјпјҢз”ЁжҲ·еҝғжҷәжЁЎеһӢжҲҗзҶҹгҖӮ

4. **Pi дҫ§дјҳеҢ–пјҲеҸҜйҖүдҪҶжҺЁиҚҗпјү**пјҡеҰӮжһң Pi SDK жңӘжқҘж”ҜжҢҒеӨҡ session е№¶еҸ‘пјҲеӨҡдёӘ `AgentSession` е®һдҫӢпјүпјҢTab еҲҮжҚўеҸҜд»Ҙе®Ңе…Ёйӣ¶е»¶иҝҹгҖӮеҪ“еүҚжһ¶жһ„зҡ„еҚ• session йҷҗеҲ¶жҳҜжңҖеӨ§зҡ„дҪ“йӘҢз“¶йўҲгҖӮ

---

**жҖ»з»“пјҡжҠҖжңҜдёҠе®Ңе…ЁеҸҜиЎҢпјҢж ёеҝғйҡҫзӮ№еңЁ `usePiRpc` зҡ„еӨҡ Tab зҠ¶жҖҒйҡ”зҰ»е’ҢжөҒејҸдәӢд»¶и·Ҝз”ұгҖӮдёӯзӯүж–№жЎҲ 6-8 еӨ©еҸҜе®ҢжҲҗпјҢжңҖеӨ§йЈҺйҷ©жҳҜ Pi `switchSession` зҡ„е»¶иҝҹеҪұе“Қ Tab еҲҮжҚўдҪ“йӘҢгҖӮ**

content.thinking: The user wants me to investigate why Pi is designed to only support one active session at a time. Let me look at the Pi SDK source code and documentation to understand this design decision.

## Pi дёәд»Җд№Ҳи®ҫи®ЎдёәгҖҢеҚ•жҙ»и·ғ SessionгҖҚпјҡж·ұеәҰи°ғз ”

### дёҖгҖҒж ёеҝғи°ғз”Ёй“ҫ

д»Һ `AgentSessionRuntime` зҡ„жәҗз ҒеҸҜд»Ҙжё…жҘҡзңӢеҲ° session еҲҮжҚўзҡ„е®Ңж•ҙжөҒзЁӢпјҡ

```
switchSession(sessionPath)
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ 1. emitBeforeSwitch("resume")        вҶҗ жү©еұ•й’©еӯҗпјҢеҸҜеҸ–ж¶Ҳ
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ 2. SessionManager.open(sessionPath)   вҶҗ жү“ејҖж–° session ж–Үд»¶
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ 3. teardownCurrent("resume")          вҶҗ й”ҖжҜҒеҪ“еүҚ session
  в”Ӯ     в”ңв”Җв”Җ emitSessionShutdownEvent()      вҶҗ йҖҡзҹҘжү©еұ• session е…ій—ӯ
  в”Ӯ     в”ңв”Җв”Җ beforeSessionInvalidate()       вҶҗ еҗҢжӯҘжё…зҗҶ UI з»„д»¶
  в”Ӯ     в””в”Җв”Җ session.dispose()              вҶҗ йҮҠж”ҫ AgentSession
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ 4. createRuntime({...})              вҶҗ йҮҚе»әж•ҙдёӘиҝҗиЎҢж—¶
  в”Ӯ     в”ңв”Җв”Җ createAgentSessionServices()    вҶҗ йҮҚе»ә cwd з»‘е®ҡжңҚеҠЎ
  в”Ӯ     в””в”Җв”Җ createAgentSessionFromServices()вҶҗ еҲӣе»әж–° AgentSession
  в””в”Җв”Җ 5. finishSessionReplacement()         вҶҗ йҮҚж–°з»‘е®ҡжү©еұ•
        в”ңв”Җв”Җ rebindSession(newSession)       вҶҗ йҮҚж–°и®ўйҳ…дәӢд»¶
        в””в”Җв”Җ withSession(ctx)               вҶҗ жҒўеӨҚдёҠдёӢж–Ү
```

### дәҢгҖҒж №жң¬еҺҹеӣ пјҡSession дёҚд»…д»…жҳҜж¶ҲжҒҜеҺҶеҸІ

дёҖдёӘ `AgentSession` з»‘е®ҡдәҶ**еӨ§йҮҸжңүзҠ¶жҖҒзҡ„иҝҗиЎҢж—¶иө„жәҗ**пјҢдёҚжҳҜз®ҖеҚ•зҡ„ж•°жҚ®е®№еҷЁпјҡ

| иө„жәҗ | з»‘е®ҡж–№ејҸ | жҳҜеҗҰеҸҜе…ұдә« |
|------|----------|-----------|
| **Agent (pi-agent-core)** | `session.agent` вҖ” зҠ¶жҖҒжңәпјҢжҢҒжңү `messages`гҖҒ`tools`гҖҒ`isStreaming`гҖҒ`pendingToolCalls`гҖҒ`steeringQueue`гҖҒ`followUpQueue` | вқҢ жҜҸдёӘ Agent еҶ…йғЁжңүдёҖдёӘ `activeRun`пјҢеҗҢж—¶еҸӘиғҪжңүдёҖдёӘиҝҗиЎҢдёӯзҡ„ LLM и°ғз”Ё |
| **SessionManager** | `session.sessionManager` вҖ” з®ЎзҗҶеҪ“еүҚ session зҡ„ JSONL ж–Үд»¶гҖҒleaf жҢҮй’ҲгҖҒentry tree | вқҢ жңү `leafId` жҢҮй’ҲзҠ¶жҖҒпјҢ`_persist()` дјҡ append еҲ°еҪ“еүҚж–Үд»¶ |
| **ExtensionRunner** | `session._extensionRunner` вҖ” жү§иЎҢжү©еұ•йҖ»иҫ‘пјҢжҢҒжңү UI дёҠдёӢж–Ү | вқҢ жү©еұ•жңү `session_shutdown`/`session_start` з”ҹе‘Ҫе‘Ёжңҹй’©еӯҗпјҢз»‘е®ҡдәҶеҪ“еүҚ session зҡ„е·Ҙе…·жіЁеҶҢ |
| **ToolRegistry** | `session._toolRegistry` вҖ” еҪ“еүҚеҗҜз”Ёзҡ„е·Ҙе…·е®ҡд№үе’Ң prompt snippets | вҡ пёҸ дҫқиө– session зҡ„жү©еұ•жіЁеҶҢ |
| **Bash иҝӣзЁӢ** | `session._bashAbortController` вҖ” еҪ“еүҚжӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢзҡ„ bash е‘Ҫд»Ө | вқҢ иҝӣзЁӢзә§иө„жәҗпјҢдёҖдёӘ session еҸӘиғҪжңүдёҖдёӘжҙ»и·ғ bash |
| **Compaction зҠ¶жҖҒ** | `session._compactionAbortController` вҖ” иҮӘеҠЁ/жүӢеҠЁеҺӢзј© | вқҢ жңүзҠ¶жҖҒзҡ„ејӮжӯҘж“ҚдҪң |
| **FileMutationQueue** | е…ЁеұҖеҚ•дҫӢ `Map<filePath, Promise>` вҖ” еәҸеҲ—еҢ–еҜ№еҗҢдёҖж–Үд»¶зҡ„е№¶еҸ‘еҶҷж“ҚдҪң | вҡ пёҸ е…ЁеұҖе…ұдә«дҪҶж“ҚдҪңзҡ„жҳҜеҗҢдёҖдёӘ cwd дёӢзҡ„ж–Үд»¶ |
| **Cwd-bound Services** | `AgentSessionServices` вҖ” `authStorage`гҖҒ`modelRegistry`гҖҒ`resourceLoader`гҖҒ`settingsManager` | вҡ пёҸ жҢү cwd з»‘е®ҡпјҢеҗҢдёҖ cwd еҸҜе…ұдә« |

### дёүгҖҒи®ҫи®Ўе“ІеӯҰеұӮйқўзҡ„еҺҹеӣ 

#### 1. **Agent Loop жҳҜзӢ¬еҚ зҡ„**

`Agent`пјҲжқҘиҮӘ `pi-agent-core`пјүжң¬иҙЁдёҠжҳҜдёҖдёӘ**еҚ•зәҝзЁӢзҠ¶жҖҒжңә**пјҡ

```typescript
class Agent {
    private activeRun?: ...  // еҗҢж—¶еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘиҝҗиЎҢдёӯзҡ„ run
    readonly steeringQueue   // дёІиЎҢж¶ҲжҒҜйҳҹеҲ—
    readonly followUpQueue   // дёІиЎҢж¶ҲжҒҜйҳҹеҲ—
}
```

Agent еҫӘзҺҜжҳҜ `prompt вҶ’ stream вҶ’ tool_call вҶ’ tool_result вҶ’ stream вҶ’ ...` зҡ„й—ӯзҺҜгҖӮеҰӮжһңдёӨдёӘ session еҗҢж—¶иҝҗиЎҢеҗ„иҮӘзҡ„ Agent loopпјҡ
- е®ғд»¬еҸҜиғҪе№¶еҸ‘еҶҷеҗҢдёҖдёӘж–Үд»¶пјҲedit/write toolпјү
- Bash е‘Ҫд»ӨеҸҜиғҪдә’зӣёе№Іжү°
- Token йў„з®—е’Ң rate limit ж— жі•еҚҸи°ғ

#### 2. **Session жҳҜ TreeпјҢдёҚжҳҜ List**

`SessionManager` з®ЎзҗҶзҡ„жҳҜдёҖдёӘ**жңүеҗ‘ж— зҺҜж ‘**пјҡ

```
root (user message 1)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ entry A (assistant reply 1)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ entry B (user message 2, fork point)
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ entry C (assistant reply 2a) вҶҗ leaf of branch A
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ entry D (assistant reply 2b) вҶҗ leaf of branch B
```

`leafId` жҢҮй’Ҳж Үи®°дәҶеҪ“еүҚеңЁж ‘зҡ„е“ӘдёӘдҪҚзҪ®гҖӮеҲҮжҚў session дёҚеҸӘжҳҜжҚўдёҖдёӘж¶ҲжҒҜеҲ—иЎЁпјҢиҖҢжҳҜеҲҮжҚўеҲ°дёҖдёӘе®Ңе…ЁдёҚеҗҢзҡ„ж ‘гҖӮдёӨдёӘ `SessionManager` е®һдҫӢеҸҜд»ҘжҢҮеҗ‘дёҚеҗҢзҡ„ж–Үд»¶пјҢдҪҶе®ғд»¬дёҚиғҪеҗҢж—¶еҫҖ**еҗҢдёҖдёӘ agent state** йҮҢиҝҪеҠ ж¶ҲжҒҜгҖӮ

#### 3. **Extension з”ҹе‘Ҫе‘Ёжңҹз»‘е®ҡ Session**

жү©еұ•зі»з»ҹжңүдёҘж јзҡ„з”ҹе‘Ҫе‘Ёжңҹпјҡ

- `session_start` вҶ’ жү©еұ•еҲқе§ӢеҢ–пјҢжіЁеҶҢе·Ҙе…·пјҢи®ҫзҪ® UI
- `session_shutdown` вҶ’ жү©еұ•жё…зҗҶпјҢдҝқеӯҳзҠ¶жҖҒ
- `session_before_switch` вҶ’ жү©еұ•еҸҜд»ҘеҸ–ж¶ҲеҲҮжҚў
- `session_before_fork` вҶ’ жү©еұ•еҸҜд»ҘеҸ–ж¶Ҳ fork

иҝҷж„Ҹе‘ізқҖжү©еұ•еҒҮи®ҫ**еҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘжҙ»и·ғ session**гҖӮеҰӮжһңеӨҡдёӘ session еҗҢж—¶жҙ»и·ғпјҢжү©еұ•зҡ„ `onSessionStart` / `onSessionShutdown` иҜӯд№үе°Ҷе®Ңе…Ёж··д№ұгҖӮ

#### 4. **ж–Үд»¶зі»з»ҹжҳҜе…ұдә«дё”дә’ж–Ҙзҡ„**

Pi зҡ„ж ёеҝғе·Ҙе…·пјҲread/edit/write/bashпјүйғҪж“ҚдҪң**еҗҢдёҖдёӘ cwd дёӢзҡ„ж–Үд»¶зі»з»ҹ**гҖӮ`FileMutationQueue` жҳҜдёҖдёӘе…ЁеұҖеҚ•дҫӢпјҢзЎ®дҝқеҜ№еҗҢдёҖж–Үд»¶зҡ„еҶҷж“ҚдҪңдёІиЎҢеҢ–гҖӮеҰӮжһңдёӨдёӘ session еҗҢж—¶иҝҗиЎҢпјҡ
- Session A `edit` foo.ts з¬¬ 10 иЎҢ
- Session B `edit` foo.ts з¬¬ 10 иЎҢ
- з»“жһңдёҚеҸҜйў„жөӢпјҢдё”еҸҜиғҪдёўеӨұдҝ®ж”№

иҝҷдёҚжҳҜжҠҖжңҜйҷҗеҲ¶иҖҢжҳҜ**иҜӯд№үйҷҗеҲ¶**пјҡдёӨдёӘ AI agent еҗҢж—¶ж”№еҗҢдёҖдёӘд»Јз Ғеә“жҳҜеҚұйҷ©зҡ„гҖӮ

### еӣӣгҖҒиҝҷжҳҜеҗҰжҳҜж°ёд№…зҡ„йҷҗеҲ¶пјҹ

**дёҚдёҖе®ҡ**пјҢдҪҶж”№еҠЁеҫҲеӨ§гҖӮPi зҡ„жһ¶жһ„е®һйҷ…дёҠе·Із»ҸдёәжӯӨеҒҡдәҶйғЁеҲҶеҮҶеӨҮпјҡ

| е·Іжңүзҡ„жҠҪиұЎ | иҜҙжҳҺ |
|-----------|------|
| `AgentSessionRuntime` | е·Із»Ҹе°Ҷ session жӣҝжҚўйҖ»иҫ‘е°ҒиЈ…дёә `teardown вҶ’ create вҶ’ apply`пјҢзҗҶи®әдёҠеҸҜд»Ҙз®ЎзҗҶеӨҡдёӘ runtime |
| `createRuntime` е·ҘеҺӮ | жҳҜдёҖдёӘзәҜеҮҪж•°пјҢз»ҷе®ҡ `cwd + sessionManager` е°ұиғҪеҲӣе»әж–° runtime |
| `SessionManager.open()` / `SessionManager.create()` | жҳҜзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„пјҢеӨҡдёӘе®һдҫӢеҸҜд»ҘеҗҢж—¶еӯҳеңЁ |

**дҪҶиҰҒж”ҜжҢҒеӨҡ session е№¶еҸ‘**пјҢйңҖиҰҒи§ЈеҶіпјҡ

1. **Agent е№¶еҸ‘жү§иЎҢ** вҖ” жҜҸдёӘ session йңҖиҰҒзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ Agent е®һдҫӢпјҢзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ LLM иҝһжҺҘпјҲеҗ„иҮӘзҡ„ API key / rate limitпјү
2. **ж–Үд»¶ж“ҚдҪңеҶІзӘҒ** вҖ” йңҖиҰҒи·Ё session зҡ„ж–Үд»¶й”ҒжҲ–ж“ҚдҪңйҳҹеҲ—
3. **Extension еӨҡе®һдҫӢ** вҖ” жү©еұ•йңҖиҰҒж”ҜжҢҒеҗҢж—¶з»‘е®ҡеӨҡдёӘ sessionпјҢжҲ–иҖ…жҜҸдёӘ session жңүзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ extension runner
4. **Bash иҝӣзЁӢйҡ”зҰ»** вҖ” жҜҸдёӘ session йңҖиҰҒзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ bash жү§иЎҢзҺҜеўғ
5. **иө„жәҗж¶ҲиҖ—** вҖ” жҜҸдёӘ session йғҪжҢҒжңүе®Ңж•ҙзҡ„ LLM context еңЁеҶ…еӯҳдёӯпјҢ10 дёӘ session = 10 еҖҚеҶ…еӯҳ

### дә”гҖҒеҜ№ Tab еҠҹиғҪзҡ„еҗҜзӨә

Pi зҡ„еҚ• session и®ҫи®Ў**дёҚжҳҜ bugпјҢжҳҜж·ұжҖқзҶҹиҷ‘зҡ„жһ¶жһ„йҖүжӢ©**пјҢеҺҹеӣ еҪ’з»“дёәдёҖзӮ№пјҡ

> **дёҖдёӘ AI agent ж“ҚдҪңдёҖдёӘд»Јз Ғеә“пјҢеҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘиғҪжңүдёҖдёӘжҙ»и·ғзҡ„жү§иЎҢдёҠдёӢж–ҮгҖӮ**

дҪҶиҝҷдёҚж„Ҹе‘ізқҖ Tab дёҚеҸҜеҒҡгҖӮдёӨз§ҚеҸҜиЎҢи·Ҝеҫ„пјҡ

#### и·Ҝеҫ„ AпјҡеүҚз«Ҝзј“еӯҳ + еҗҺз«ҜдёІиЎҢеҲҮжҚўпјҲжҺЁиҚҗпјү

```
еүҚз«Ҝ Tab зј“еӯҳжҜҸдёӘ session зҡ„ messages
еҲҮжҚў Tab ж—¶пјҡ
  1. д»Һзј“еӯҳжҒўеӨҚ UIпјҲеҚіж—¶пјү
  2. еҗҺеҸ°и°ғ switchSessionпјҲејӮжӯҘпјү
  3. switchSession е®ҢжҲҗеҗҺеҲ·ж–°зј“еӯҳ
```

- дёҚйңҖиҰҒж”№ Pi SDK
- дҪ“йӘҢпјҡйҰ–ж¬Ўжү“ејҖ Tab жңүе»¶иҝҹпјҢеӣһеҲ°е·Іжү“ејҖзҡ„ Tab еҚіж—¶
- йҷҗеҲ¶пјҡеҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘ session еҸҜд»Ҙе’Ң LLM дәӨдә’

#### и·Ҝеҫ„ BпјҡеӨҡ Pi Worker иҝӣзЁӢ

```
жҜҸдёӘжҙ»и·ғ Tab еҜ№еә”дёҖдёӘзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ pi-worker иҝӣзЁӢ
иҝӣзЁӢй—ҙйҖҡиҝҮ main process дёӯиҪ¬ IPC
```

- жҜҸдёӘ worker жҢҒжңүзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ `AgentSession` + `Agent` + `ExtensionRunner`
- зңҹжӯЈзҡ„е№¶иЎҢпјҡTab A streaming ж—¶еҸҜд»ҘеҲҮеҲ° Tab B еҸ‘ prompt
- д»Јд»·пјҡеҶ…еӯҳзҝ»еҖҚгҖҒAPI е№¶еҸ‘ж¶ҲиҖ—гҖҒж–Үд»¶еҶІзӘҒйЈҺйҷ©
- йңҖиҰҒи·Ё worker зҡ„ FileMutationQueueпјҲиҝӣзЁӢзә§иҖҢйқһиҝӣзЁӢеҶ…зә§пјү

**и·Ҝеҫ„ A зҡ„д»Јд»·жңҖе°ҸпјҢд№ҹжҳҜеӨ§еӨҡж•° AI IDEпјҲCursorгҖҒWindsurfпјүзҡ„еҒҡжі•гҖӮ**

content.thinking:
The user is clarifying that they don't need parallel execution (two sessions running LLM calls simultaneously), but rather concurrent Tab switching - like browser tabs where only one is "active" at a time, but you can switch between them instantly because their state is cached.

This is exactly "и·Ҝеҫ„ A" from my analysis - frontend caching + backend serial switching. The key insight is:

1. The UI shows multiple tabs, each representing a session
2. Only one tab is "active" - the one currently connected to Pi
3. Switching tabs should feel instant because messages are cached on the frontend
4. The backend (Pi) still only has one active session at a time, but switching is done lazily/async

So the question becomes much simpler - it's really just about frontend caching and making tab switching feel snappy. Let me re-evaluate the feasibility and work estimate with this understanding.

Let me think about what actually needs to change:

1. **Tab bar UI** - new component, straightforward
2. **Multi-session message caching** - the core change. Instead of one `messages[]` in `usePiRpc`, we need a `Map<sessionPath, TabState>` that caches messages for each opened tab
3. **Tab switching flow**:
   - User clicks Tab B
   - Frontend immediately shows Tab B's cached messages (instant)
   - Backend calls `switchSession(B)` (async, 200-500ms)
   - When switch completes, refresh Tab B's cache with latest data from Pi
4. **Streaming across tabs** - If Tab A is streaming and user switches to Tab B:
   - Pi events for Tab A still arrive
   - We need to route them to Tab A's cache (not display them on Tab B)
   - Tab A's tab indicator shows a streaming animation
5. **Input bar** - only the active tab can send prompts

This is much more feasible than I initially framed it. The Pi single-session constraint isn't really a blocker at all - it's just about smart frontend caching.

Let me re-assess the work needed more concisely.

content.text:
жҳҺзҷҪдәҶпјҢдҪ иҰҒзҡ„е°ұжҳҜ**еүҚз«Ҝзј“еӯҳ + еҗҺз«ҜдёІиЎҢеҲҮжҚў**вҖ”вҖ”и·ҹжөҸи§ҲеҷЁ Tab дёҖж ·пјҢеҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘ activeпјҢдҪҶеҲҮжҚўжҳҜеҚіж—¶зҡ„пјҢеӣ дёәзҠ¶жҖҒзј“еӯҳеңЁеүҚз«ҜгҖӮ

йӮЈ Pi еҚ• session зҡ„йҷҗеҲ¶**ж №жң¬дёҚжҳҜйҡңзўҚ**гҖӮйҮҚж–°иҜ„дј°пјҡ

## дҝ®жӯЈеҗҺзҡ„ж–№жЎҲ

```
Tab A (active, streamingдёӯ)    Tab B (cached, idle)    Tab C (cached, idle)
        в”Ӯ
        в””в”ҖвҶ’ з”ЁжҲ·зӮ№ Tab B
```

The user is asking a very important question: if Tab A is currently streaming (Pi is generating a response), and the user switches to Tab B, what happens to the streaming in Tab A?

Let me think about this carefully by tracing through the code.

When the user switches Tab:
1. Frontend immediately shows Tab B's cached content
2. Backend calls `switchSession(targetPath)` which does:
   - `emitBeforeSwitch("resume")` - extension hook
   - `teardownCurrent("resume")` - which calls `session.dispose()`
   - `createRuntime({...})` - creates new session

So `switchSession` will **destroy the current session** and create a new one. This means:
- The current Agent loop (if streaming) will be disposed
- The streaming will stop
- Any partial response will be lost

Let me verify by looking at the teardown flow again:

```javascript
async teardownCurrent(reason, targetSessionFile) {
    await emitSessionShutdownEvent(this.session.extensionRunner, {...});
    this.beforeSessionInvalidate?.();
    this.session.dispose();  // This disposes the AgentSession
}
```

And `session.dispose()` in AgentSession:
```javascript
dispose() {
    this._unsubscribeAgent?.();
    // removes all listeners
}
```

And the Agent's `dispose` would abort any running operation.

So yes, switching sessions via `switchSession` WILL stop any streaming in the current session.

This is a real problem for the Tab design. The user's expectation would be:
- Tab A is streaming вҶ’ user switches to Tab B to read something вҶ’ switches back to Tab A вҶ’ streaming should still be going

But with `switchSession`, the streaming in Tab A would be aborted.

The user is asking exactly this question. Let me be honest about this.

Options to handle this:
1. **Don't call switchSession while streaming** - Just show cached content for Tab B, but don't switch the Pi backend. This means Tab B is read-only until Tab A finishes streaming. This is how most AI IDEs work.

2. **Abort streaming on switch** - Call switchSession which aborts the current stream. The partial response is saved (Pi saves messages on agent_end). When user switches back, they see the partial response.

3. **Queue the switch** - Wait for streaming to finish, then switch. Show cached content immediately but defer the backend switch.

Option 1 is the most practical and matches what Cursor/Claude Code do. You can view other sessions but can't interact with them while one is streaming.

Actually, let me reconsider. When Pi is streaming:
- Events are being pushed to the frontend
- The Agent loop is running

If we don't call `switchSession` when switching to Tab B:
- We show Tab B's cached messages immediately
- Pi continues streaming for Tab A in the background
- We route the streaming events to Tab A's cache (not to the visible UI)
- When user switches back to Tab A, we show the updated cache
- When streaming finishes, user can then interact with Tab B

This is actually the most natural behavior. The only issue is:
- User can't send prompts to Tab B while Tab A is streaming
- But they CAN read Tab B's history, scroll, etc.

This is exactly how browser tabs work when one is loading - you can switch and read other tabs.

The key insight: **we should NOT call switchSession when switching tabs if the current session is streaming**. We only call switchSession when the user actually wants to interact with a different session (send a prompt).

So there are two types of tab switches:
1. **Passive switch** (just viewing): Don't call switchSession. Show cached content. Route ongoing events to the correct tab cache.
2. **Active switch** (want to interact): If current session is streaming, either wait or abort. Then call switchSession.

Actually, this is getting complex. Let me think about what's simplest and most correct.

The simplest correct answer to the user's question: **Yes, if you call `switchSession`, the streaming will be aborted.** Because `switchSession` calls `teardownCurrent` which calls `session.dispose()` which unsubscribes from the agent.

So the design needs to account for this. The best approach is:
- When switching tabs, DON'T immediately call switchSession
- Only call switchSession when the user tries to send a prompt to a different session
- While just viewing, keep Pi connected to the current session

The user is asking: can we implement "switch to session B while session A continues running in the background" with the current architecture (without Tab UI)? They want to discuss the feasibility before coding.

Let me think about this carefully.

The current flow when switching sessions:
1. `switchSession(sessionPath)` in `AgentSessionRuntime`
2. This calls `teardownCurrent()` which calls `session.dispose()` - this kills the current Agent loop
3. Then creates a new runtime for the target session

So the fundamental question is: **can we keep session A's Agent running while switching to session B?**

Let me trace through the code more carefully.

In `pi-worker.ts`:
- There's ONE global `session` and ONE global `runtime`
- `switchSession` replaces the global `session` with a new one
- The old session is disposed

In `AgentSessionRuntime.switchSession()`:
- `teardownCurrent()` вҶ’ `session.dispose()` вҶ’ unsubscribes from agent events, agent keeps running? No, let me check.

Actually, let me re-read `session.dispose()`:

From `agent-session.d.ts`:
```
dispose(): void;
```

And from the class definition:
```
private _unsubscribeAgent?;
...
dispose(): void; // Removes all listeners and disconnects from agent
```

So `dispose()` just unsubscribes the event listener from the Agent. The Agent itself might still be running (if it's in the middle of streaming).

But wait - when you call `teardownCurrent`, you:
1. Emit session_shutdown event
2. Call `session.dispose()` which unsubscribes from agent events
3. Then create a new runtime

The problem is: the `Agent` instance is part of `AgentSession`. When you create a new `AgentSession`, you get a new `Agent`. The old `Agent` is still running but nobody is listening to its events.

But actually, the deeper issue is that the old Agent is still running in the background. It's consuming API calls, executing tools, etc. If we then create a new AgentSession and start prompting it, we'd have two Agent loops running concurrently, which means:
- Two concurrent LLM API calls (wasteful, rate limit issues)
- Two concurrent tool executions (file conflicts)
- The old Agent's tool executions might conflict with the new Agent's

So the question becomes: can we let session A "run in the background" without it interfering?

The answer depends on what "continues to run" means:

1. **If session A is streaming an LLM response (no tool calls yet)**: The Agent is just receiving tokens. This is relatively safe - it's just consuming tokens and appending to the message state. But there's no way to "pause" the streaming and resume later.

2. **If session A is executing tool calls**: This is dangerous - the tools are modifying the filesystem, running bash commands, etc.

3. **If session A is in a "follow-up" or "steering" loop**: Again, could be executing tools.

Actually, wait. Let me re-think this. The user is asking about the "еүҚеҗҺз«ҜеҲҶзҰ»" (front-backend separation) approach. What they mean is:

Currently:
- pi-worker (backend) holds one session
- renderer (frontend) displays it

What if:

- renderer switches to display session B's cached history

The key question is: **can the pi-worker hold onto session A while also switching to session B?**

Looking at the pi-worker code:
```typescript

let runtime: AgentSessionRuntime | null = null
```

These are singletons. To support "session A keeps running while session B is active", we'd need:

Option 3: Don't actually switch in Pi - just fake it in the frontend
- Frontend caches messages for session A
- Frontend "switches" to session B by loading B's history from the JSONL file

Wait, option 3 is interesting but has a problem: the frontend can read session B's JSONL file for history, but it can't send prompts to session B without switching Pi's connection.

They want: switch to session B, but session A continues running (streaming).

The fundamental problem: `AgentSessionRuntime.switchSession()` calls `teardownCurrent()` which disposes the old session, killing the streaming.

2. We'd need to keep the old one alive somewhere

But the real constraints are:
1. **File system**: If session A's tools are editing files, and session B also needs to edit files, they conflict. But if session A is just streaming (receiving LLM tokens, no tool calls yet), it's safe.

2. **LLM API**: Two concurrent API calls = more tokens consumed. But this is a cost issue, not a correctness issue.

3. **Extensions**: The ExtensionRunner is per-session. Having two extension runners might cause issues if they both try to modify UI state. But in the Electron GUI, the UI is in the renderer, not the worker.

So actually, for the "session A continues streaming" case specifically, it IS feasible:
- Session A's Agent is in the middle of receiving LLM tokens (no tool calls)
- We create session B's runtime alongside session A
- We keep both alive
- Events from session A are routed to the "A" cache
- Events from session B are routed to the "B" cache

But when session A's current turn finishes and it has tool calls to execute... that's where it gets tricky. The tools would need to execute in the context of session A, which might conflict with session B.

Actually, the simplest and most pragmatic approach would be:

**Don't keep session A's Agent running. Instead, let session A finish its current turn naturally, and then switch.**

Or even simpler:

**The "session A continues running" doesn't mean the Agent keeps executing. It means the Pi worker stays connected to session A until the current turn completes. The frontend just shows session B's cached history in the meantime.**

Wait, but the user specifically asked about "session A з»§з»ӯи·‘" (session A continues running). Let me think about what this means in practice.

When the user switches from Tab A to Tab B:
- Tab A might be in the middle of receiving a response
- The user wants that response to complete even though they're looking at Tab B
- When they switch back to Tab A later, they should see the complete response

For this to work, the Pi worker needs to:
1. Keep session A's Agent loop running (receiving tokens, executing tools)
2. Buffer the events from session A
3. When the user switches back, replay/merge those events

This is basically Option 1: multiple runtime instances in the same worker.

But actually, there's an even simpler approach that doesn't require changing the Pi SDK at all:

**Use the frontend-only caching approach with delayed switchSession.**

Here's the flow:

1. Tab A is streaming
2. User clicks Tab B
3. Frontend immediately shows Tab B's cached content (from JSONL file read)
4. Pi worker stays on session A, continues streaming
5. Pi events for session A are cached by the frontend (written to Tab A's cache)
6. When session A finishes streaming (agent_end event), then automatically call switchSession(Tab B)
7. OR when user tries to interact with Tab B, abort A and switch

This approach:
- Doesn't require two AgentSession instances
- Doesn't require changing the Pi SDK
- Session A completes its current turn naturally
- Only one Pi session is "active" at any time
- The "switch" to session B is deferred until session A is done

The key insight: **we don't need to switch Pi immediately. We just need to show the user session B's history while session A finishes.**

Actually, this is what the user is asking about. They said "еүҚеҗҺз«ҜеҲҶзҰ»" - the frontend shows session B, the backend continues running session A.

But wait, there's still a problem. If the user wants to SEND a message to session B while session A is still streaming... that requires switching Pi. And switching Pi kills session A's streaming.

So the realistic UX would be:
- Switching to VIEW session B while A streams: вң… works (frontend only)
- Sending a message to session B while A streams: вқҢ need to abort A first

Let me think about whether we can avoid aborting A...

Actually, could we have two AgentSession instances in the pi-worker? Let me look at what resources they'd share vs. conflict:

Shared resources that would conflict:
- File system (edit/write/bash tools) - both agents could modify the same files
- Extensions (ExtensionRunner) - extensions might not support being bound to two sessions
- UI state - but UI is in the renderer, not the worker

Separate resources:
- Agent instances (each has its own state, messages, tools)
- SessionManager instances (each points to a different JSONL file)
- API connections (each makes its own HTTP requests)

The file system conflict is the real issue. If agent A is in the middle of editing foo.ts, and agent B also tries to edit foo.ts, the results are unpredictable.

But... if agent A is just streaming (receiving LLM tokens), it's not touching the file system. The conflict only arises when tools execute.

So a hybrid approach:
1. While agent A is just receiving tokens (streaming): safe to have B active too
2. When agent A starts executing tools: either wait for A to finish, or abort A

Hmm, but the Agent loop is: prompt вҶ’ stream вҶ’ [tool_call вҶ’ tool_result вҶ’ stream вҶ’ [tool_call вҶ’ ...]] вҶ’ agent_end

During the "stream" phase, no tools are executing. During the "tool" phase, tools are executing. The phases interleave within a single agent turn.

So if A is in a stream phase, B could safely run. But when A starts a tool execution, we'd need to... pause? abort?

This is getting complicated. Let me step back and think about what the user actually needs.

The user's core need: **when I switch away from a streaming session, the response should complete. When I come back, I should see the full response.**

The simplest way to achieve this:
1. Don't switch Pi when the user just wants to VIEW another session
2. Keep the Pi events flowing and cache them
3. Only switch Pi when the user wants to INTERACT with another session

And for the "user wants to interact with another session while A is streaming" case:
- Abort A (save partial response to cache)
- Switch to B
- Send the prompt

This is a good UX compromise. The user can always come back to A and see whatever was received before the abort.

OK so now the question is more specific: can we implement the "Pi stays on A, frontend shows B" pattern without Tab UI? The user said "tabзҡ„еүҚз«Ҝе…ҲдёҚз®Ў" - let's ignore the Tab UI for now.

The answer is: **yes, this is purely a frontend change.** The Pi worker doesn't need to change at all. Here's why:

Current flow:
1. Pi is connected to session A
2. User clicks session B in sidebar
3. Frontend calls `switchSession(B)` вҶ’ Pi tears down A, creates B
4. Frontend clears messages, loads B's history

Proposed flow:
1. Pi is connected to session A, streaming
2. User clicks session B in sidebar
3. Frontend:
   a. Saves current messages to cache for A
   b. Reads B's history from JSONL file (via IPC)
   c. Displays B's history
   d. Does NOT call switchSession yet
   e. Pi events for A continue arriving, get cached for A
4. When user tries to interact with B (send prompt), THEN call switchSession

Wait, but there's a problem with step 3e. The Pi events are currently being handled by `usePiRpc` which directly updates `setMessages`. If the frontend is "showing" session B but Pi is still connected to A, the events would try to update the displayed messages with session A's events.

So we need:
- A way to cache events for the "background" session
- A way to show a different session's history
- The Pi worker stays unchanged

This is essentially what the Tab cache design would do. But the user said "tabзҡ„еүҚз«Ҝе…ҲдёҚз®Ў" - let's think about what the minimum viable implementation looks like.

Minimum viable: When switching sessions, if the current session is streaming:
1. Keep the Pi connection on the current session
2. Continue receiving and caching events (in a ref, not in displayed state)
3. Load the target session's history from file
4. Display the target session's history
5. Show some indicator that "Pi is still running in session A"
6. When Pi finishes (agent_end), then call switchSession

But wait, this means the user sees session B's history but can't interact with it (can't send prompts). That's a degraded UX.

Actually, the user is asking a simpler question than I'm thinking about. Let me re-read:

"жҲ‘зҗҶи§ЈиҝҷеҸҜд»ҘеүҚеҗҺз«ҜеҲҶзҰ»пјҢеңЁзҺ°еңЁзҡ„жЁЎејҸдёӢд№ҹеҸҜд»Ҙе®һзҺ°пјҢtabзҡ„еүҚз«Ҝе…ҲдёҚз®Ўзҡ„иҜқпјҢиғҪе®һзҺ°еҲҮжҚў sessionпјҢsession Aз»§з»ӯи·‘зҡ„ж•Ҳжһңеҗ§пјҹ"

They're asking: **can we implement "switch to session B, session A keeps running" in the current architecture (without Tab UI)?**

I think the answer is: **not without changing the pi-worker**, because `switchSession` in `AgentSessionRuntime` always tears down the current session.

But wait, we could change the pi-worker to support this. The pi-worker is our code, not Pi SDK code.

In the pi-worker, instead of:
```typescript
let session: AgentSession | null = null
let runtime: AgentSessionRuntime | null = null
```

We could have:
```typescript
const sessions: Map<string, AgentSession> = new Map()
let activeSessionPath: string | null = null
```

But `AgentSessionRuntime` doesn't support this. The `switchSession` method always tears down the current one.

We could bypass `AgentSessionRuntime` and manage `AgentSession` instances directly. But that means reimplementing the session lifecycle that `AgentSessionRuntime` handles (extension bindings, session_start/shutdown events, etc.).

OR... we could use multiple `AgentSessionRuntime` instances. But each one holds a reference to `session`, and the `createRuntime` factory is designed to be called once and then reused.

Actually, let me think about this differently. The user is asking if the current Pi SDK supports this. The answer is: **no, not directly, because `switchSession` disposes the old session.** But we can work around it in the pi-worker layer.

Option A: Don't call switchSession until the current turn is done
- Frontend shows session B's history (read from JSONL file)
- Pi stays on session A
- Events for A are cached
- When A finishes or user explicitly wants to interact with B, then switch

Option B: Create a second runtime without disposing the first
- Modify pi-worker to hold two runtimes
- Keep runtime A alive while creating runtime B
- Route events correctly

Option A is simpler and doesn't require changing the pi-worker's architecture. It's the "front-end only" approach.

Option B is more powerful but requires significant changes to the pi-worker.

I think the user is asking about whether Option A is feasible. And the answer is: yes, but the devil is in the details of event routing.

Let me think about the event routing problem more carefully.

Currently, `usePiRpc` subscribes to Pi events via `window.api.onEvent()`. These events are for the currently connected session. When we "virtually" switch to session B but Pi is still on A:

1. The renderer displays session B's messages
2. Pi events for session A arrive
3. These events should NOT update the displayed messages
4. They should be cached for later

When the user switches back to session A:
1. Display the cached messages (including new events from the background streaming)
2. If Pi is still streaming on A, the events continue arriving and now update the display

This is doable. The key change is in `usePiRpc`: instead of directly updating the displayed `messages`, events go into a per-session cache, and only the active session's cache is reflected in the displayed state.

But actually, there's another problem. When the user is looking at session B and Pi is still on A:
- The user sees B's history (loaded from JSONL)
- But B might have been modified since (unlikely unless another process is writing to B's file)
- The user can't send prompts to B without switching Pi
- The InputBar should be disabled or show a "session A is busy" message

This is getting into the UX design territory. The user said "е…ҲдёҚиҰҒcodingпјҢи®Ёи®әжё…жҘҡ" - they want to discuss, not code.

Let me structure my response around the key question: **can we have "switch to B, A keeps running" without Tab UI?**

Yes, but the implementation is essentially the same Tab cache logic without the Tab bar UI. The core challenge is the same: event routing and state isolation.

The pi-worker doesn't need to change for this. The change is entirely in the renderer:
1. `usePiRpc` needs to support per-session caching
2. The session switch logic needs to be "lazy" (don't switch Pi until needed)
3. The UI needs to indicate that the active Pi session is different from the displayed session

Actually, wait. Let me reconsider. If we're just talking about the frontend caching approach (Option A), there's actually a subtle but important issue:

**When the user switches to session B while A is streaming, we need to load B's history. But we can't call `getMessages` on Pi (that returns A's messages). We need to read B's JSONL file directly.**

Currently, `loadHistory` calls `api.getMessages()` which goes to the pi-worker which calls `session.messages`. This always returns the current (A's) messages.

So we need a new IPC channel: `session:getMessagesForSession(sessionPath)` that reads a JSONL file without switching Pi.

Or we could use the existing `session-service.ts` in the main process to parse the JSONL file and return messages. But `session-service.ts` currently only returns `SessionInfo` (metadata), not the full message content.

So we'd need to either:
1. Add a `getMessagesForSession(sessionPath)` method to the session service
2. Or add a new IPC channel that asks the pi-worker to temporarily open a session file and read its messages

Option 1 is simpler and doesn't involve the pi-worker at all. The main process can read the JSONL file, parse it, and return the messages. This is essentially what `loadHistory` does but without Pi.

But there's a complication: the message format from `session-service.ts` would be different from what `usePiRpc.loadHistory()` returns. The `loadHistory` function processes Pi's internal message format into `ChatMessage[]`. We'd need to do the same conversion for the JSONL-parsed messages.

Actually, looking at the session-service.ts more carefully, it only parses metadata (name, messageCount, status, etc.), not the full message content. We'd need to add full message parsing.

OK let me structure my response now.

The key points:
1. Yes, it's feasible
2. The core is "front-end cache + lazy Pi switch"
3. Pi worker doesn't need to change (mostly)
4. We need a way to read other sessions' history without switching Pi
5. Event routing in usePiRpc is the main challenge
6. The UX for "can't send messages while Pi is busy on another session" needs design

```
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
```

## йңҖиҰҒи§ЈеҶізҡ„е…·дҪ“й—®йўҳ

### й—®йўҳ 1пјҡжҖҺд№ҲиҜ» B зҡ„еҺҶеҸІж¶ҲжҒҜпјҹ

### й—®йўҳ 2пјҡA зҡ„ streaming дәӢд»¶жҖҺд№Ҳзј“еӯҳпјҹ

### й—®йўҳ 3пјҡB зҡ„ InputBar жҖҺд№ҲеӨ„зҗҶпјҹ

| **е…Ғи®ё** | иҫ“е…ҘеҗҺе…Ҳ abort A вҶ’ switchSession(B) вҶ’ еҸ‘йҖҒ | зҒөжҙ» | дёӯж–ӯ A зҡ„е“Қеә” |

жҺЁиҚҗ**е…Ғи®ёдҪҶз»ҷжҸҗзӨә**пјҡInputBar жӯЈеёёеҸҜз”ЁпјҢдҪҶеңЁеҸ‘йҖҒеүҚеј№дёҖдёӘзЎ®и®Ө"Session A жӯЈеңЁиҝҗиЎҢпјҢжҳҜеҗҰдёӯж–ӯе№¶еҲҮжҚўпјҹ"

### й—®йўҳ 4пјҡPi иҝһжҺҘзҠ¶жҖҒе’ҢжҳҫзӨәзҠ¶жҖҒзҡ„еҲҶзҰ»

зҺ°еңЁдёӨиҖ…жҳҜз»‘е®ҡзҡ„гҖӮйңҖиҰҒжӢҶжҲҗдёӨдёӘжҰӮеҝөпјҡ

```
piConnectedSession: string    // Pi worker е®һйҷ…иҝһжҺҘзҡ„ sessionпјҲеҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘпјү
displayedSession: string      // еүҚз«ҜеҪ“еүҚжҳҫзӨәзҡ„ session
```

UI дёҠеә”иҜҘи®©з”ЁжҲ·зҹҘйҒ“дёӨиҖ…зҡ„е…ізі»гҖӮжҜ”еҰӮ header жҳҫзӨәпјҡ

```
еҪ“еүҚжҹҘзңӢ: Session B (еҸӘиҜ»)        вҶҗ displayedSession !== piConnectedSession
еҪ“еүҚжҹҘзңӢ: Session B               вҶҗ displayedSession === piConnectedSession
```

## йңҖиҰҒж”№зҡ„ж–Үд»¶

| ж–Үд»¶ | ж”№еҠЁ | еұӮзә§ |
|------|------|------|
| `session-service.ts` | ж–°еўһ `parseSessionMessages(filePath)` ж–№жі• | main |
| `main/index.ts` | ж–°еўһ IPC handler `session:getMessagesForSession` | main |
| `preload/index.ts` | жҡҙйңІж–° IPC | preload |
| `usePiRpc.ts` | дәӢд»¶и·Ҝз”ұеҲ°зј“еӯҳиҖҢйқһзӣҙжҺҘ setMessages | renderer |
| `App.tsx` | еҲҶзҰ» piConnectedSession / displayedSession | renderer |
| `ChatView.tsx` | еҹәжң¬дёҚж”№ | renderer |
| `InputBar.tsx` | еҠ "еҸӘиҜ»"зҠ¶жҖҒжҸҗзӨә | renderer |
| **pi-worker.ts** | **дёҚж”№** | вҖ” |
| **Pi SDK** | **дёҚж”№** | вҖ” |

## з»“и®ә

**еҸҜиЎҢпјҢдё” Pi worker е’Ң Pi SDK йғҪдёҚйңҖиҰҒж”№гҖӮ** ж”№еҠЁе…ЁеңЁ main processпјҲеҠ  JSONL и§Јжһҗпјүе’Ң rendererпјҲзј“еӯҳ + е»¶иҝҹеҲҮжҚўпјүгҖӮжң¬иҙЁдёҠжҳҜжҠҠ"еҲҮжҚў session"д»Һ**еҗҢжӯҘйҳ»еЎһж“ҚдҪң**еҸҳжҲҗ**ејӮжӯҘе»¶иҝҹж“ҚдҪң**пјҢеҗҢж—¶еүҚз«Ҝз”Ёзј“еӯҳдҝқиҜҒеҚіж—¶е“Қеә”гҖӮ

иҝҷдёӘж–№жЎҲи·ҹ Tab UI жҳҜжӯЈдәӨзҡ„вҖ”вҖ”е…ҲеҒҡе»¶иҝҹеҲҮжҚўпјҢеҗҺйқўеҠ  Tab ж Ҹе°ұжҳҜзәҜ UI еұӮзҡ„дәӢгҖӮ

# Spec: Lazy Session SwitchпјҲе»¶иҝҹдјҡиҜқеҲҮжҚўпјү

## 1. еҠЁжңә

еҪ“еүҚеҲҮжҚў session жҳҜдёҖдёӘ**еҗҢжӯҘйҳ»еЎһзҡ„з ҙеқҸжҖ§ж“ҚдҪң**пјҡ

```
switchSession(B)
  вҶ’ teardownCurrent() вҶ’ session.dispose()   // A зҡ„ Agent иў«й”ҖжҜҒ
  вҶ’ createRuntime(B)                         // B зҡ„ Runtime иў«еҲӣе»ә
```

иҝҷеҜјиҮҙдёӨдёӘй—®йўҳпјҡ

1. **еҲҮжҚўжңүе»¶иҝҹ**пјҡ`switchSession` + `loadHistory` ж¶үеҸҠ Pi RPC еҫҖиҝ”пјҲ200-500msпјүпјҢжңҹй—ҙ UI йҳ»еЎһжҲ–з©әзӘ—
2. **жӯЈеңЁ streaming зҡ„ session дјҡиў«дёӯж–ӯ**пјҡз”ЁжҲ·еҲҮеҲ° B жҹҘзңӢпјҢA зҡ„жөҒејҸе“Қеә”зӣҙжҺҘеҒңжӯў

зӣ®ж Үпјҡе®һзҺ°**е»¶иҝҹеҲҮжҚў**пјҢеүҚз«ҜжҳҫзӨәе’Ң Pi иҝһжҺҘи§ЈиҖҰпјҢи®© session еҲҮжҚўеҸҳжҲҗйқһйҳ»еЎһж“ҚдҪңгҖӮ

## 2. ж ёеҝғжҰӮеҝө

### 2.1 дёӨдёӘзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„зҠ¶жҖҒ

| жҰӮеҝө | иҜҙжҳҺ | ж•°йҮҸ |
|------|------|------|
| **piConnectedSession** | Pi worker е®һйҷ…иҝһжҺҘзҡ„ sessionпјҢеҗҢдёҖж—¶еҲ»еҸӘжңүдёҖдёӘ | 1 |
| **displayedSession** | еүҚз«ҜеҪ“еүҚеұ•зӨәзҡ„ sessionпјҢеҸҜд»ҘжңүзӢ¬з«Ӣзҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜзј“еӯҳ | 1пјҲжңӘжқҘ Tab жЁЎејҸдёӢдёә Nпјү |

еҪ“ `displayedSession !== piConnectedSession` ж—¶пјҢdisplayedSession еӨ„дәҺ**еҸӘиҜ»жЁЎејҸ**пјҡ
- еҸҜд»ҘжҹҘзңӢеҺҶеҸІж¶ҲжҒҜгҖҒж»ҡеҠЁ
- дёҚиғҪзӣҙжҺҘеҸ‘йҖҒ promptпјҲйңҖе…ҲеҲҮжҚў Pi иҝһжҺҘпјү
- дёҚдјҡж”¶еҲ°е®һж—¶ streaming дәӢд»¶

### 2.2 Session зј“еӯҳ

жҜҸдёӘжӣҫжү“ејҖиҝҮзҡ„ session з»ҙжҠӨдёҖд»ҪеүҚз«Ҝзј“еӯҳпјҡ

```typescript
interface SessionCache {
  sessionPath: string
  messages: ChatMessage[]
  isStreaming: boolean
  streamingMessageId: string | null
  tokenUsage: TokenUsage
  forkPoints: ForkPoint[]
  loadedAt: number  // зј“еӯҳеҠ иҪҪж—¶й—ҙжҲіпјҢз”ЁдәҺеҲӨж–ӯжҳҜеҗҰйңҖиҰҒеҲ·ж–°
}
```

### 2.3 еҲҮжҚўзӯ–з•Ҙ

| еңәжҷҜ | Pi зҠ¶жҖҒ | ж“ҚдҪң |
|------|---------|------|
| жҳҫзӨә AпјҲidleпјүвҶ’ еҲҮеҲ° B | A idle | з«ӢеҚіжҳҫзӨә B зј“еӯҳ + еҗҺеҸ° switchSession(B) |
| жҳҫзӨә AпјҲstreamingпјүвҶ’ еҲҮеҲ° B жҹҘзңӢ | A streaming | з«ӢеҚіжҳҫзӨә B зј“еӯҳпјҢ**дёҚи°ғ switchSession**пјҢA з»§з»ӯи·‘ |
| еңЁ B иҫ“е…ҘжЎҶеҸ‘ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҢдҪҶ Pi иҝһеңЁ A | A еҸҜиғҪ streaming | е…Ҳ abort(A) вҶ’ switchSession(B) вҶ’ еҸ‘йҖҒ |
| A streaming е®ҢжҲҗ | A idle | еҰӮжһң displayedSession вү  piConnectedSessionпјҢеҸҜеҗҺеҸ° switchSession |
| еӣһеҲ° A | A еҸҜиғҪе·І idle | жҳҫзӨә A зј“еӯҳпјҲе·Іеҗ« streaming з»“жһңпјүпјҢеҰӮ Pi иҝһеңЁеҲ«еӨ„еҲҷеҗҺеҸ°еҲҮеӣһ |

## 3. ж•°жҚ®жөҒ

### 3.1 жӯЈеёё streamingпјҲPi иҝһжҺҘ = жҳҫзӨәпјү

```
Pi Worker                         Renderer
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  agent_start                   в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  вҶ’ еҶҷе…Ҙ sessionCache[A].isStreaming = true
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ    еҲ·ж–° UIпјҲеӣ дёә A === displayedSessionпјү
   в”Ӯ  message_start (user)          в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  вҶ’ append to sessionCache[A].messages
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  message_update (text_delta)   в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  вҶ’ update sessionCache[A].messages
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
```

### 3.2 Streaming дёӯеҲҮжҚўжҹҘзңӢ B

```
Pi Worker                         Renderer
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  [A streaming дёӯ]               в”Ӯ  displayedSession = A
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  з”ЁжҲ·зӮ№еҮ» B
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 1. saveCache(A)                  в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 2. displayedSession = B           в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 3. restoreCache(B) жҲ– loadFromJSONL в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ 4. дёҚи°ғ switchSession             в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  message_update (text_delta)   в”Ӯ  displayedSession = B вү  piConnectedSession = A
   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  вҶ’ йқҷй»ҳеҶҷе…Ҙ sessionCache[A].messages
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  вҶ’ дёҚеҲ·ж–° UIпјҲеӣ дёә A вү  displayedSessionпјү
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  вҶ’ Tab/ж Үйўҳ дёҠ A жҳҫзӨә рҹ”„пјҲеҰӮжһңжңү Tab UIпјү
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  agent_end                     в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  вҶ’ sessionCache[A].isStreaming = false
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ  вҶ’ еҰӮжһң displayedSession === Bпјҡ
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ     еҸҜйҖүпјҡеҗҺеҸ° switchSession(B)
```

### 3.3 еңЁ B еҸ‘ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҲPi иҝһеңЁ Aпјү

```
Renderer                          Pi Worker
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  з”ЁжҲ·еңЁ B иҫ“е…ҘжЎҶеҸ‘йҖҒ              в”Ӯ  displayedSession = B, piConnectedSession = A
   в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ if A is streaming:       в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   abort()               в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ switchSession(B)        в”Ӯв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  teardown A вҶ’ createRuntime B
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ loadHistory()           в”Ӯ<в”Җв”Җв”Ӯ  иҝ”еӣһ B зҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ refreshCache(B)         в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ
   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ sendPrompt(text)        в”Ӯв”Җв”Җ>в”Ӯ  prompt in B
   в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
```

## 4. иҜ»еҸ–йқһжҙ»и·ғ Session зҡ„еҺҶеҸІ

еҪ“ Pi иҝһжҺҘеңЁ AпјҢз”ЁжҲ·жғізңӢ B зҡ„еҺҶеҸІж—¶пјҢдёҚиғҪиө° `getMessages` RPCпјҲиҝ”еӣһзҡ„жҳҜ A зҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜпјүгҖӮ

### 4.1 ж–№жЎҲпјҡmain process зӣҙжҺҘи§Јжһҗ JSONL

`session-service.ts` е·Іжңү `parseSessionFile()` и§Јжһҗ header + metadataгҖӮж–°еўһж–№жі•и§Јжһҗе®Ңж•ҙж¶ҲжҒҜеҲ—иЎЁпјҡ

```
ж–°еўһ IPC: session:getMessagesForSession(sessionPath)
  вҶ’ main/index.ts
    вҶ’ session-service.parseSessionMessages(sessionPath)
      вҶ’ иҜ»еҸ– JSONL ж–Үд»¶
      вҶ’ йҒҚеҺҶ entriesпјҢжһ„е»ә ChatMessage[]
      вҶ’ иҝ”еӣһз»ҷ renderer
```

жӯӨж“ҚдҪңжҳҜзәҜж–Үд»¶иҜ»еҸ–пјҢдёҚж¶үеҸҠ Pi workerпјҢдёҚи§ҰеҸ‘д»»дҪ• session еҲҮжҚўгҖӮ

### 4.2 ж¶ҲжҒҜж јејҸиҪ¬жҚў

`parseSessionMessages` йңҖиҰҒжҠҠ Pi зҡ„еҺҹе§Ӣ entry ж јејҸиҪ¬жҚўдёә renderer зҡ„ `ChatMessage` ж јејҸгҖӮиҝҷдёӘиҪ¬жҚўйҖ»иҫ‘зӣ®еүҚеӯҳеңЁдәҺ `usePiRpc.ts` зҡ„ `loadHistory()` дёӯпјҢйңҖиҰҒжҸҗеҸ–дёәе…ұдә«еҮҪж•°гҖӮ

### 4.3 зј“еӯҳеҲ·ж–°ж—¶жңә

JSONL ж–Үд»¶и§Јжһҗеҫ—еҲ°зҡ„жҳҜ**зЈҒзӣҳдёҠзҡ„еҝ«з…§**пјҢеҸҜиғҪдёҺ Pi еҶ…еӯҳдёӯзҡ„жңҖж–°зҠ¶жҖҒдёҚеҗҢжӯҘгҖӮеҲ·ж–°ж—¶жңәпјҡ

| ж—¶жңә | ж“ҚдҪң |
|------|------|
| йҰ–ж¬Ўжү“ејҖ session | д»Һ JSONL еҠ иҪҪ |
| Pi иҝһжҺҘеҲҮжҚўеҲ°иҜҘ session | `loadHistory()` иҰҶзӣ–зј“еӯҳпјҲеҶ…еӯҳжңҖж–°пјү |
| йқһжҙ»и·ғ session ж”¶еҲ° `agent_end` | еҸҜйҖүпјҡд»Һ JSONL йҮҚж–°еҠ иҪҪпјҲеӣ дёә Pi е·І flush еҲ°зЈҒзӣҳпјү |
| еӣһеҲ°жҹҗ session дё” Pi дёҚеңЁиҜҘ session | д»Һ JSONL йҮҚж–°еҠ иҪҪ |

## 5. API еҸҳжӣҙ

### 5.1 ж–°еўһ main process IPC

```typescript
// session:getMessagesForSession
// д»Һ JSONL ж–Үд»¶и§ЈжһҗжҢҮе®ҡ session зҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҲдёҚз»ҸиҝҮ Pi workerпјү
ipcMain.handle('session:getMessagesForSession', async (_event, sessionPath: string) => {
  return sessionService.parseSessionMessages(sessionPath)
})
```

### 5.2 ж–°еўһ session-service ж–№жі•

```typescript
// session-service.ts
export function parseSessionMessages(filePath: string): ChatMessage[] {
  // 1. иҜ»еҸ– JSONL ж–Үд»¶
  // 2. и§Јжһҗ entries
  // 3. жһ„е»ә ChatMessage[]пјҲеӨҚз”Ё renderer зҡ„иҪ¬жҚўйҖ»иҫ‘пјү
  // 4. иҝ”еӣһ
}
```

### 5.3 preload жҡҙйңІ

```typescript
getMessagesForSession: (sessionPath: string) => Promise<ChatMessage[]>
```

### 5.4 дёҚж”№зҡ„йғЁеҲҶ

- **pi-worker.ts**пјҡдёҚж”№гҖӮPi worker з»§з»ӯеҚ• session жЁЎеһӢгҖӮ
- **Pi SDK**пјҡдёҚж”№гҖӮ`switchSession` иҜӯд№үдёҚеҸҳгҖӮ
- **ChatView.tsx**пјҡдёҚж”№гҖӮз»§з»ӯжҺҘж”¶ `messages` props жёІжҹ“гҖӮ
- **SessionSidebar.tsx**пјҡдёҚж”№гҖӮзӮ№еҮ»д»Қи§ҰеҸ‘ `onSwitchSession`пјҢдҪҶеӣһи°ғиЎҢдёәеҸҳеҢ–гҖӮ

## 6. Renderer еұӮзҠ¶жҖҒз®ЎзҗҶ

### 6.1 ж–°еўһ hook: useSessionCache

д»Һ `usePiRpc` дёӯжӢҶеҲҶеҮәзј“еӯҳйҖ»иҫ‘пјҡ

```typescript
interface UseSessionCacheReturn {
  // еҪ“еүҚжҳҫзӨәзҡ„ session
  displayedSessionPath: string | null
  // Pi иҝһжҺҘзҡ„ session
  piConnectedSessionPath: string | null
  // еҪ“еүҚжҳҫзӨәзҡ„ж¶ҲжҒҜпјҲжқҘиҮӘзј“еӯҳпјү
  displayedMessages: ChatMessage[]
  // еҪ“еүҚжҳҫзӨә session жҳҜеҗҰдёәеҸӘиҜ»
  isReadOnly: boolean  // displayedSessionPath !== piConnectedSessionPath
  // еҪ“еүҚжҳҫзӨә session жҳҜеҗҰжӯЈеңЁ streaming
  isDisplayedStreaming: boolean
  // еҲҮжҚўжҳҫзӨәпјҲдёҚеҲҮжҚў Pi иҝһжҺҘпјү
  displaySession: (sessionPath: string) => Promise<void>
  // зЎ®дҝқ Pi иҝһжҺҘеҲ°жҢҮе®ҡ sessionпјҲз”ЁдәҺеҸ‘йҖҒж¶ҲжҒҜеүҚпјү
  ensurePiConnected: (sessionPath: string) => Promise<void>
  // еҸ‘йҖҒ promptпјҲиҮӘеҠЁеӨ„зҗҶиҝһжҺҘеҲҮжҚўпјү
  sendPromptWithSession: (sessionPath: string, text: string, images?: ...) => Promise<void>
}
```

# Session Management Spec

## 1. Overview

Session management allows users to organize conversations with the Pi agent into named, resumable sessions. Each project (working directory) gets a tree of sessions rooted at a "main" session. Users can fork at any message, switch between sessions, rename, delete, and navigate back to parent sessions.

## 2. Storage Format

### 2.1 Session Directory

```
~/.pi/agent/sessions/
  --<encoded-cwd>--/
    <timestamp>_<uuid>.jsonl
    <timestamp>_<uuid>.jsonl
  --<another-project>--/
    <timestamp>_<uuid>.jsonl
```

- `encoded-cwd`: the working directory with `/` replaced by `-`, wrapped in `--`. E.g. `/Users/foo/project` вҶ’ `--Users-foo-project--`
- Each project directory contains one or more `.jsonl` session files
- When the last session file in a project directory is deleted, the directory is removed

### 2.2 Session File (JSONL)

Each line is a JSON object. The first line is always the session header.

```
{"type":"session","version":3,"id":"<uuid>","timestamp":"<ISO>","cwd":"<path>","parentSession":"<parent-filepath>"}
{"type":"message","id":"<entryId>","parentId":"<parentEntryId>","timestamp":"<ISO>","message":{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}}
{"type":"message","id":"<entryId>","parentId":"<parentEntryId>","timestamp":"<ISO>","message":{"role":"assistant","content":[...]}}
{"type":"session_info","name":"my-experiment"}
{"type":"fork_point","entryId":"<entryId>","childName":"fork-name"}
```

**Header** (line 0):
| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `type` | `"session"` | Always `"session"` |
| `version` | `3` | Session format version |
| `id` | `string` | UUID for this session |
| `timestamp` | `string` | ISO timestamp of creation |
| `cwd` | `string` | Working directory |
| `parentSession` | `string?` | File path of parent session (for forks) |

**Entry types** (lines 1+):
| `type` | Purpose | Key Fields |
|--------|---------|------------|
| `message` | A conversation entry | `id`, `parentId`, `message.role`, `message.content` |
| `session_info` | Session metadata (name, status) | `name`, `status` |
| `fork_point` | Records where a fork was created | `entryId`, `childName` |

**Name resolution**: If multiple `session_info` entries exist, the **last** one wins for each field (rename overwrites).

**Status resolution**: Same as name вҖ” if multiple `session_info` entries contain `status`, the **last** one wins. If no `session_info` entry contains `status`, the session is considered `active`.

### 2.3 Inter-Session Relationships

Sessions form a tree via the `parentSession` header field:

```
main.jsonl          (no parentSession)
  в”ңв”Җв”Җ fork-a.jsonl  (parentSession: "/path/to/main.jsonl")
  в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ sub.jsonl (parentSession: "/path/to/fork-a.jsonl")
  в””в”Җв”Җ fork-b.jsonl  (parentSession: "/path/to/main.jsonl")
```

## 3. Core Concepts

### 3.1 Main Session

- Each project has exactly one **main session**
- The main session is identified by:
  1. A session explicitly named `"main"`, OR
  2. The oldest session for the project (fallback)
- On app startup, the main session is automatically resumed via `--session <path>` flag to PiBridge
- If the main session has no name, it is automatically named `"main"` before PiBridge starts
- `isMain` is set in `listSessions()` based on the session name, **not** the currently active session
- The currently active session is tracked separately by `currentSession?.filePath` in the frontend

### 3.2 Session Naming

- **All new sessions and forks require user-provided names** вҖ” no auto-naming
- Unnamed sessions display their creation time (e.g. "May 29 16:24") instead of "Untitled"
- Names are persisted by appending `{"type":"session_info","name":"..."}` to the JSONL file
- For new sessions: name is set via `set_session_name` RPC, then `flush_session` writes the file with all entries (header + session_info) to disk. `nameSession()` is NOT called for new sessions to avoid conflicting with Pi's lazy-flush mechanism
- For existing sessions (rename): name is set via `set_session_name` RPC, then `nameSession()` appends session_info to the already-flushed file as a fallback

### 3.3 Fork

A fork creates a new session that shares conversation history up to a specific message (entry point), then diverges.

**Fork flow**:
1. User clicks "Fork" on a user message вҶ’ ForkPopover opens
2. User selects which message to fork from (entry point)
3. User enters a fork name вҶ’ confirms
4. Main process:
   - Records `parentPath` from current `get_state` RPC
   - Sends `fork` RPC with `entryId` to Pi
   - Sends `set_session_name` RPC with user name
   - Writes `nameSession(childPath, name)` to disk
   - Writes `addForkPoint(parentPath, entryId, name)` to parent's JSONL
5. Frontend:
   - `clearMessages()` вҶ’ `forkAtEntry(entryId, name)` вҶ’ `loadHistory()` вҶ’ `refresh()`

### 3.4 Fork Points

When a fork is created, a `fork_point` entry is appended to the **parent** session's JSONL file. This allows the UI to show visual markers on messages where forks originated.

```
{"type":"fork_point","entryId":"entry-42","childName":"experiment-1"}
```

- `entryId`: the `id` field from the Pi message entry where the fork occurred
- `childName`: the name the user gave to the forked session

Fork points are rendered as purple badges on the corresponding message in ChatView.

### 3.5 Session Status (Completed)

Each session can be marked as **completed** to indicate the user is done with it and likely won't revisit it soon.

- **Status values**: `active` (default) or `completed`
- **Storage**: Appended as `{"type":"session_info","status":"completed"}` to the JSONL file, reusing the existing `session_info` entry type
- **Resolution**: Last `session_info` entry's `status` field wins; absent `status` means `active`
- **Auto-recovery**: **None** вҖ” sending a message to a completed session does NOT automatically change it back to `active`. Only manual toggle.
- **Operations on completed sessions**: All operations (switch, fork, delete, rename) work the same regardless of status
- **Visual**: Completed sessions appear with gray text (`text-gray-400`) and strikethrough (`line-through`) in the sidebar; dot turns gray (`bg-gray-300 border-gray-300`). No separate section вҖ” completed sessions stay in their tree position.
- **Sorting**: No change вҖ” completed sessions remain in their original position in the tree; no grouping or reordering
- **Child sessions**: A completed session's children are unaffected; they may be active or completed independently

### 3.6 Delete

- Sessions can be deleted from the sidebar
- **Active sessions can be deleted** вҖ” the backend creates a new session first, then deletes the old one, similar to `clearSession`
- **Cannot delete main sessions** вҖ” UI hides the delete button
- When the last session in a project directory is deleted, the project directory is removed
- Delete is a two-step confirmation: hover shows `x`, click shows red `Del`, click again executes

### 3.6 New Child Session

Each session node in the sidebar has a **"+"** button (visible on hover). Clicking it creates a **child session** under that session. The child session is blank (no conversation history), unlike a fork which copies history.

**New child session flow (sidebar)**:
1. User clicks "+" button on any session node вҶ’ inline name input appears
2. User types a session name вҶ’ presses Enter (or clicks Create button)
3. Frontend calls `onNewSession(name, parentSessionPath)` where `parentSessionPath` is the clicked session's file path
4. Main process: `new_session` RPC (with parentSession) вҶ’ `set_session_name` RPC вҶ’ `flush_session` RPC
5. The new session becomes active and appears in the sidebar as a child of the parent session

**Why `flush_session` is needed**: Pi's `newSession()` only creates the session in memory (deferred file write). The file is not written until the first assistant response. `flush_session` calls `sessionManager._rewriteFile()` + sets `flushed=true`, which: (1) writes the file with the correct header (including `parentSession`), and (2) prevents Pi's subsequent `_persist` from using `openSync('wx')` which would fail on an existing file. This mirrors what `createBranchedSession` does when it has assistant messages.

### 3.7 Fork

When a session is active, a **Fork** button appears in the sidebar session node. Clicking it creates a new session that forks from the **last user message** in the current conversation вҖ” i.e., the new session shares the entire conversation history and then diverges.

**Fork flow (sidebar)**:
1. User clicks Fork button on the active session node вҶ’ inline name input appears
2. User types a fork name вҶ’ presses Enter (or clicks Fork button)
3. Frontend finds the last user message's `piEntryId` from the messages array
4. Frontend calls `forkAtEntry(lastUserEntryId, name)` вҖ” same RPC flow as regular fork
5. The new session becomes active and appears in the sidebar as a child of the original session

This is a shortcut for the most common fork use case вҖ” forking from the end of the conversation. The existing ForkPopover (fork from any message) remains available in ChatView.

### 3.8 Session Status Toggle

Users can toggle a session between `active` and `completed` via the right-click context menu:

- If the session is `active` вҶ’ menu shows **"Mark as completed"**
- If the session is `completed` вҶ’ menu shows **"Mark as active"**

The toggle writes a new `session_info` entry to the JSONL file and refreshes the sidebar. No RPC call to Pi is needed вҖ” this is a pure file operation.

## 4. Data Flow

### 4.1 Backend (Main Process)

#### Session Service (`session-service.ts`)

Pure functions operating on the filesystem. All functions that access the session directory accept an optional `sessionDir` parameter for testability.

| Function | Signature | Description |
|----------|-----------|-------------|
| `getSessionDir()` | `() вҶ’ string` | Returns `~/.pi/agent/sessions` |
| `decodeProjectDir()` | `(encodedDir) вҶ’ string` | `--Users-foo-bar--` вҶ’ `/Users/foo/bar` |
| `parseSessionFile()` | `(filePath) вҶ’ SessionInfo \| null` | Parse header + entries from JSONL |
| `findMainSession()` | `(cwd, sessionDir?) вҶ’ SessionInfo \| null` | Find named "main" or oldest for cwd |
| `listSessions()` | `(currentSessionPath?, sessionDir?) вҶ’ SessionListResult` | List all sessions grouped by project |
| `buildSessionTree()` | `(sessions) вҶ’ SessionTreeNode \| null` | Build parent-child tree from SessionInfo[] |
| `nameSession()` | `(sessionPath, name) вҶ’ boolean` | Append session_info entry to existing JSONL; no-op if file doesn't exist |
| `setSessionStatus()` | `(sessionPath, status: 'active' \| 'completed') вҶ’ boolean` | Append session_info entry with status to JSONL |
| `deleteSession()` | `(sessionPath, sessionDir?) вҶ’ boolean` | Delete file + cleanup empty directory |
| `addForkPoint()` | `(sessionPath, entryId, childName) вҶ’ boolean` | Append fork_point entry to JSONL |
| `getForkPoints()` | `(sessionPath) вҶ’ ForkPoint[]` | Extract all fork_point entries from JSONL |

#### IPC Handlers (`index.ts`)

| Channel | RPC Commands | Notes |
|---------|-------------|-------|
| `session:listSessions` | `get_state` (unused for isMain) вҶ’ `listSessions()` | `isMain` based on name, not currentSessionPath |
| `session:getForkMessages` | `get_fork_messages` | Returns forkable user messages |
| `session:forkAtEntry` | `get_state` вҶ’ `fork` вҶ’ `set_session_name` вҶ’ `get_state` | Records fork point in parent |
| `session:switchSession` | `switch_session` | Passes sessionPath |
| `session:newSession` | `new_session` вҶ’ `set_session_name` вҶ’ `flush_session` | Passes parentSession; flush ensures file written with correct header |
| `session:renameSession` | `set_session_name` + `nameSession()` | RPC first, file fallback always |
| `session:getCurrentSession` | `get_state` вҶ’ `listSessions()` вҶ’ find | Matches by sessionPath |
| `session:deleteSession` | `get_state` (check if active) вҶ’ if active: `new_session` + `deleteSession()`; else: `deleteSession()` | Handles active session deletion by creating new session first |
| `session:getMessages` | `get_messages` | Returns raw Pi message array |
| `session:getForkPoints` | (no RPC) вҶ’ `getForkPoints()` | Reads directly from disk |
| `session:setSessionStatus` | (no RPC) вҶ’ `setSessionStatus()` | Pure file operation; no Pi RPC needed |
| `session:softPeek` | `soft_peek` | Reads messages from session without switching runtime (В§6.6.7) |
| `session:getRuntimeSessionId` | `get_state` | Returns sessionId + sessionFile + isStreaming of runtime's current session |

#### RPC Correlation

All RPC commands use UUID correlation via `pendingRpcResponses` Map with 30s timeout:

```
sendRpcCommand({ type: "fork", entryId }) 
  вҶ’ piBridge.sendCommand({ ...command, id: randomUUID() })
  вҶ’ Promise pending in Map
  вҶ’ response arrives with matching id вҶ’ resolve/reject
```

### 4.2 IPC Bridge (Preload)

| Channel | Direction | Payload |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| `session:listSessions` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |
| `session:getForkMessages` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |
| `session:forkAtEntry` | renderer вҶ’ main | `entryId: string, name?: string` |
| `session:switchSession` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string` |
| `session:newSession` | renderer вҶ’ main | `name: string, parentSessionPath?: string` |
| `session:renameSession` | renderer вҶ’ main | `name: string` |
| `session:getCurrentSession` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |
| `session:refreshSessions` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |
| `session:getMessages` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |
| `session:deleteSession` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string` |
| `session:getForkPoints` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string` |
| `session:setSessionStatus` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string, status: 'active' \| 'completed'` |
| `session:softPeek` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string` |
| `session:getRuntimeSessionId` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) |

### 4.3 Frontend

#### Hooks

**`usePiRpc`** вҖ” manages real-time Pi communication and message state:
- `messages: ChatMessage[]` вҖ” current conversation (streaming or loaded)
- `forkPoints: ForkPoint[]` вҖ” fork markers for current session
- `loadHistory()` вҖ” fetches `get_messages` RPC, converts Pi вҶ’ ChatMessage format
- `loadForkPoints(sessionPath)` вҖ” fetches fork points for a session
- `clearMessages()` вҖ” resets messages and forkPoints

**`useSessionManager`** вҖ” manages session CRUD:
- `sessions: SessionListResult | null` вҖ” all projects/sessions
- `currentSession: SessionInfo | null` вҖ” active session (from RPC)
- `forkAtEntry(entryId, name)` вҖ” fork with user-provided name
- `switchSession(sessionPath)` вҖ” switch to different session
- `newSession(name, parentSessionPath?)` вҖ” create child session with name under specified parent
- `deleteSession(sessionPath)` вҖ” delete non-active session
- `setSessionStatus(sessionPath, status)` вҖ” toggle session active/completed
- `refresh()` вҖ” reload sessions + currentSession

#### Conversion: Pi Messages вҶ’ ChatMessage

`loadHistory()` converts Pi's raw message format into the UI's ChatMessage format:

| Pi Message | ChatMessage |
|------------|-------------|
| `{ role: "user", content: "text" }` | `{ role: "user", blocks: [{ type: "text", content: "text" }] }` |
| `{ role: "user", content: [{ type: "text", text: "..." }] }` | Concatenated text blocks |
| `{ role: "assistant", content: [{ type: "text" }] }` | `{ role: "assistant", blocks: [{ type: "text" }] }` |
| `{ role: "assistant", content: [{ type: "thinking" }] }` | Text block with рҹ’ӯ prefix |
| `{ role: "assistant", content: [{ type: "toolCall" }] }` | ToolCallBlock with status "completed" |
| `{ role: "toolResult", content: [...] }` | **Appended** to last assistant message |

Key behaviors:
- **Tool results are NOT separate messages** вҖ” they append blocks to the current assistant message
- `piEntryId` is extracted from `msg.id` for fork point matching
- Timestamps fall back to `Date.now()` if missing

#### Active Session Name (Title Bar)

The title bar displays the current session name. Resolution order:

1. `activeSessionPath` state (set immediately on click) вҶ’ lookup in `sessions` list
2. `currentSession?.name` (from `getCurrentSession` RPC)
3. Null (nothing displayed)

This ensures the name updates instantly on click without waiting for RPC round-trips.

For unnamed sessions, `getDisplayName()` shows creation time: `"May 29 16:24"`

## 6. Lifecycle Events

### 6.1 App Startup

```
1. findMainSession(cwd)
2. If main exists but unnamed вҶ’ nameSession(path, "main")
3. initPiBridge(mainSession?.filePath)  вҶҗ passes --session flag
4. PiBridge.start() вҶ’ waits for connected event
5. If still no named "main" вҶ’ set_session_name RPC + nameSession fallback
6. Frontend: loadSessions() + loadCurrentSession()
```

### 6.2 Session Switch

```
1. setActiveSessionPath(path)  вҶҗ instant title bar update
2. clearMessages()
3. switchSession(path)  вҶҗ RPC: switch_session
4. loadHistory()        вҶҗ RPC: get_messages вҶ’ convert вҶ’ setMessages
5. loadForkPoints(path) вҶҗ IPC: getForkPoints вҶ’ setForkPoints
6. refresh()            вҶҗ reload sessions + currentSession
```

On Pi connect, fork points are loaded automatically via `useEffect` that watches `isConnected` + `currentSession?.filePath`.

### 6.3 Fork

```
1. clearMessages()
2. forkAtEntry(entryId, name)  вҶҗ IPC: fork + rename + addForkPoint (atomic in main)
3. loadHistory()               вҶҗ RPC: get_messages вҶ’ convert вҶ’ setMessages
4. refresh()                   вҶҗ reload sessions + currentSession
5. setActiveSessionPath(null)  вҶҗ let currentSession from RPC take over
```

### 6.4 Delete

```
1. User clicks x вҶ’ confirmDelete = true
2. User clicks Del вҶ’ onDelete(filePath)
3. IPC: deleteSession(path)
   - If session is active: backend creates new session first, then deletes old one
   - If session is not active: directly deletes the session file
4. On success: loadSessions() + loadCurrentSession() + refresh()
```

### 6.5 Pi Connected Event

```
onStateChanged({ connected: true })
  вҶ’ loadSessions()
  вҶ’ loadCurrentSession()
```

### 6.6 Streaming State During Session Switch

Session switch may occur while the agent is streaming. The architecture uses a **single `AgentSessionRuntime`** that can only hold one active `AgentSession` at a time. When the user switches away from a streaming session, the agent **must continue running in the background** вҖ” it must NOT be aborted.

This requires a **Soft Switch** mechanism that changes the frontend view without changing the runtime's active session. The runtime only switches when the session is idle (Hard Switch).

#### 6.6.1 Two Switch Modes

| | Soft Switch | Hard Switch |
|---|---|---|
| Trigger | User clicks a different session while current session is streaming | User clicks a different session while current session is idle |
| Runtime | **No** `runtime.switchSession()` вҖ” the old session keeps streaming | Calls `runtime.switchSession()` вҖ” runtime switches to target |
| Old session | Continues streaming in background; events are buffered | N/A (was idle) |
| Can send prompt | **No** вҖ” runtime is still on the old session; input bar is disabled | Yes |
| UI indicator | Banner: "Session X еҗҺеҸ°иҝҗиЎҢдёӯ" + Stop button | Normal |
|еҲҮеӣһжқҘ | Reload messages from the session; in-progress assistant turn is shown | Normal loadHistory |

**Why Soft Switch does not call `runtime.switchSession()`**: That function calls `session.dispose()` вҶ’ `agent.abort()`, which kills the streaming. Soft Switch avoids this entirely by leaving the runtime on the old session and only changing what the frontend displays.

#### 6.6.2 Frontend State Machine

The frontend tracks two pieces of state for soft switching:

```
viewSessionPath: string | null   вҖ” which session the user is LOOKING AT
runtimeSessionPath: string | null вҖ” which session the runtime is ON (may differ during soft switch)
```

When `viewSessionPath !== runtimeSessionPath`, the frontend is in **soft-switched mode**.

```
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ   Normal     в”Ӯ viewSession === runtimeSession
                    в”Ӯ   Mode       в”Ӯ Can send prompts, streaming events rendered
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ User clicks different session while streaming
                           в–ј
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ Soft-Switch  в”Ӯ viewSession вү  runtimeSession
                    в”Ӯ Mode         в”Ӯ Cannot send prompts; background events buffered
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
                           в”Ӯ User clicks back to runtimeSession OR agent ends
                           в–ј
                    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                    в”Ӯ   Normal     в”Ӯ
                    в”Ӯ   Mode       в”Ӯ
                    в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Exiting soft-switch mode**:

1. **User clicks back to the runtime session**: Frontend sets `viewSessionPath = runtimeSessionPath`, replays buffered events, resumes rendering.
2. **Agent ends (background streaming completes)**: `agent_end` event arrives вҶ’ frontend updates `runtimeSessionPath` to match `viewSessionPath` (the user is now looking at a different idle session), and calls `runtime.switchSession(viewSessionPath)` as a hard switch.
3. **User clicks Stop**: Aborts the background agent вҶ’ `agent_end` fires вҶ’ same as case 2.

#### 6.6.3 Event Buffering and Session ID Tagging

All events from the worker must include a `sessionId` field so the frontend can filter them during soft-switch mode.

**Worker change** вҖ” `forwardEvent` attaches `sessionId`:

```
function forwardEvent(event: AgentSessionEvent): void {
  send({ channel: 'event', data: { ...event, sessionId: session.sessionId } })
}
```

**Main process** вҖ” pass through `sessionId` unchanged (already a passthrough).

**Frontend event handler**:

```
handleEvent(event):
  if viewSessionPath === runtimeSessionPath:
    // Normal mode вҖ” process all events as before
    processEvent(event)
  else:
    // Soft-switch mode
    if event.sessionId === runtimeSessionId:
      // Event from the background (still-streaming) session
      bufferEvent(event)    // store for replay if user switches back
    else:
      // Event from an unexpected session вҖ” discard
      ignore
```

**Buffer structure**:

```
backgroundEventBuffer: AgentSessionEvent[]   // capped at 500 events
```

The buffer is cleared when soft-switch mode exits.

#### 6.6.4 Soft Switch Flow

**Entering soft switch** (user clicks session B while A is streaming):

```
1. viewSessionPath = B.path
2. loadHistoryForView(B.path)     // RPC: get_messages for session B
   // Uses new soft_peek command (see В§6.6.7) вҖ” reads messages without switching runtime
3. loadForkPoints(B.path)
4. refresh()
5. // runtimeSessionPath remains A.path; A continues streaming
6. // Input bar disabled, banner shown
```

**User clicks back to A while A still streaming**:

```
1. viewSessionPath = A.path = runtimeSessionPath  // back to normal mode
2. replayBufferedEvents()          // apply buffered deltas to current messages
3. // Input bar still disabled (streaming), but deltas now render in real-time
```

**Agent ends while in soft-switch mode** (background session A finishes):

```
1. agent_end event arrives with sessionId = A.sessionId
2. bufferEvent(agent_end)          // buffer it
3. runtimeSessionPath = viewSessionPath (B.path)  // now we can hard-switch
4. runtime.switchSession(B.path)   // hard switch to what user is looking at
5. clearBuffer()
6. syncStreamingState()            // should be false
7. // Input bar enabled, banner removed
```

**User clicks Stop while in soft-switch mode**:

```
1. Send abort command to runtime (which is on session A)
2. agent_end arrives вҶ’ same as "Agent ends" flow above
```

#### 6.6.5 Hard Switch Flow (Non-Streaming)

When the current session is NOT streaming, a session click is a hard switch вҖ” identical to the current behavior:

```
1. viewSessionPath = B.path
2. runtimeSessionPath = B.path
3. clearMessages()
4. result = switchSession(B.path)    // RPC: switch_session
5. if result.success:
     await loadHistory()
     await loadForkPoints(B.path)
     await refresh()
6. else:
     viewSessionPath = prevPath
     runtimeSessionPath = prevPath
```

#### 6.6.6 Other Operations During Soft-Switch Mode

| Operation | Allowed? | Behavior |
|-----------|----------|----------|
| New session | вқҢ | Disabled вҖ” must stop background agent first or switch back |
| Fork | вқҢ | Disabled вҖ” same reason |
| Clear session | вқҢ | Disabled вҖ” same reason |
| Delete a session (not the background one) | вҡ пёҸ | Allowed вҖ” only deletes the JSONL, doesn't affect runtime |
| Delete the background session | вқҢ | Disabled вҖ” cannot delete a session the runtime is using |

When the user tries a disabled operation, show a toast: "иҜ·е…ҲеҒңжӯўеҗҺеҸ°иҝҗиЎҢзҡ„дјҡиҜқ"

#### 6.6.7 New Worker Command: `soft_peek`

The frontend needs to read messages from a session WITHOUT switching the runtime. This is used by soft switch to display the target session's history.

```
case 'soft_peek': {
  // Read messages from a different session file without switching the runtime
  const targetPath = cmd.sessionPath as string
  const targetSm = pi.SessionManager.open(targetPath)
  const entries = targetSm.getEntries()
  const messages = entries
    .filter(e => e.type === 'message' && e.message)
    .map(e => ({ ...e.message, id: e.id }))
  send({
    channel: 'response',
    id: cmd.id,
    command: 'soft_peek',
    success: true,
    data: { messages, sessionId: entries[0]?.sessionId }
  })
  break
}
```

This creates a temporary `SessionManager` for reading, does NOT affect the runtime's active session, and does NOT call `bindSession()`.

#### 6.6.8 New IPC Channels

| Channel | Direction | Payload | Description |
|---------|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `session:softPeek` | renderer вҶ’ main | `sessionPath: string` | Read messages without switching runtime |
| `session:getRuntimeSessionId` | renderer вҶ’ main | (none) | Get the runtime's current sessionId |

Main process handlers:

```
ipcMain.handle('session:softPeek', async (_event, sessionPath: string) => {
  return piBridge.sendRpcCommand({ type: 'soft_peek', sessionPath })
})

ipcMain.handle('session:getRuntimeSessionId', async () => {
  const data = await piBridge.sendRpcCommand({ type: 'get_state' })
  return { sessionId: data.sessionId, sessionFile: data.sessionFile, isStreaming: data.isStreaming }
})
```
